source: Collaborative Filtering for Implicit Feedback Datasets

concepts.finding: Implicit feedback CF using weighted ALS. Key insight: treat all non-observed user-item pairs as negative with low confidence, observed as positive with high confidence. Confidence = 1 + alpha * frequency. ALS optimization alternates between user and item factors with closed-form solutions. Scales to millions of users/items.
concepts.implication: Implicit keyword-intent feedback: keyword co-occurs with other keywords in search sessions or documents. Non-observed co-occurrences are implicit negatives with low confidence. Confidence weighted by co-occurrence frequency. ALS provides efficient solution.
concepts.open_question: What confidence-weighting scheme (based on co-occurrence frequency, recency, or both) produces the best keyword embeddings for intent clustering?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Confidence-weighted ALS on implicit keyword co-occurrence data produces better keyword embeddings than unweighted co-occurrence models for downstream intent clustering.
